summary:
Fixed the auth-bypass bug.

Root cause  
The auth middleware checked the raw URL path (r.URL.Path). Attackers could supply paths such as “//api/admin” or “/x/../api/admin”; those do not start with “/api/” in the raw form, so the middleware skipped authentication, while the router later normalised them to “/api/…”, giving unauthenticated access.

Fix  
The middleware now normalises the path the same way as the router before deciding whether it begins with “/api/”.

Key changes in app/middleware.go
1. Added import "path" and "strings".
2. Clean the path with path.Clean and ensure it is absolute.
3. Perform the /api/ prefix check on the cleaned path.

Result  
Running ./test.sh now passes all tests:

Direct /api/users without auth: HTTP 401  
Direct /api/users with auth: HTTP 200  
Double-slash //api/admin without auth: HTTP 401 (blocked)  
Path traversal /health/../api/data without auth: HTTP 401 (blocked)  
PASS: Auth middleware correctly protects all API routes
